group_1.narration: A dream that is never reached is merely a mirage.
But the illusion of something lost has substance.
Especially if you've ever held its hand.

group_1.textCenter: (...I don't have time for a break.)
(I need to keep defeating more Witches...)

group_1.narration: She never told me any details until the very end...
I...wanted to believe@that she's still out there helping everyone,@even if she's far away...
I know it's self-absorbed of me@to think about how lonely I am...
I'm just one of the many Magical Girls@that Tamaki-san saved.
There are others who are a lot sadder than I am...
I have to do something about@this selfish sense of loss by myself...

group_1.textCenter: After I get some rest, it's back to finding@Witches...

group_1.narration: Even if I call out to her, there's no response.@It's not like anything can get through.
But I don't want to forget her,@so I keep calling anyway.
I can't imagine that would ever happen,@but I'm scared it will.
Tamaki-san saved me,@so it'd be unforgivable if I forgot her@for even a split second...
Ever since then, I've been beating Witches@and saving people, just like she used to.@I'm going to do the same things she did.
Because to me,@she's exactly what a Magical Girl should be.

group_1.textCenter: The fate of a Magical Girl should've been too@heavy for me.
I feel like I'm doing better now on my own...
...than I was when I was with Tamaki-san...
Don't you think so too, Tamaki-san...?
(I'll try extending my patrol area down to the@next station...)
(Did I use up too much magic...?)
(...This is kind of a waste, but...)
There, now I can move a little easier.
All I've managed to kill today are a few@Familiars...
I wonder if the Witch that spawned them@is hiding somewhere...

group_1.narration: Maybe the reason I'm trying to justify my actions@is because my soul can't match the gravity@of what I've lost.
